MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-29-2010 Off Calendar

Defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Nov-05-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
James P. Nevin, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL, appearing for plaintiff and opposing party Joseph Riley

Nicole Cage, Esq. of TUCKER ELLIS & WEST, appearing for defendant and moving party Rockwell Automation, Inc.

RULING:
Defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to negligence, strict liability and punitive damages and granted as to false representation. Plaintiff's declaration and deposition and the depositions of Mr. Germain create triable issuee whether plaintiff worked with internal components of new Allen-Bradley products and whether defendant acted with malice or oppression. Plaintiff's discovery responses were factually devoid and he failed to present evidence creating a triable issue that any actionable representation was made by defendant. Both the Order re Defendant Rockwell Automation Inc.'s Evidentiary Objections to Plaintiff's Evidence and Order Denying Defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment and Granting in part Summary Adjudication signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Lavena Ward, CSR #7077 = (220/HEK)
